mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.0% within a single month, it signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger corporate earnings revisions downward, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the cross-sectional predictive power drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts indicates a synchronized demand-supply mismatch that compresses corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined index of INDPRO and HOUST fails to predict forward returns with a directional accuracy exceeding 55% over any 5-year out-of-sample period.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening monetary cycle, rising consumer price inflation erodes real disposable income and reduces consumption demand, which suppresses equity valuations and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero when CPIAUCSL and RSAFS decline simultaneously during periods of rising FEDFUNDS.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real gross domestic product signals expanding aggregate demand that directly supports corporate revenue growth, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDPC1 growth and 5-day forward SPX returns falls below 0.10 over any 10-year sample, or predictive accuracy drops below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: When total nonfarm payrolls exceed 200,000 in a single month while the unemployment rate simultaneously falls, it confirms robust labor demand that fuels consumer spending and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative or flat forward return distribution in more than 40% of historical instances, or the out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of constrained industrial capacity, a sustained rise in the industrial production index signals supply-side recovery that alleviates input cost pressures and boosts corporate margins, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative when INDPRO growth exceeds the trigger threshold, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous decline in real personal savings and a contraction in housing starts indicates a liquidity crunch that restricts consumer credit availability, forcing a sharp repricing of equity risk premiums and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to precede negative SPX returns in more than 50% of observed instances, or the forward return spread between stressed and normal regimes is statistically insignificant.
